in_state.note: Tuition is guaranteed for 4 years by cohort. Fees can still change annually.

out_of_state.note: Out-of-state tuition inferred from UIC's own Lake County award matching language.

program_differentials_per_credit.note: Some programs have higher per-credit rates than the standard estimate.

medicine.note: Plus mandatory fees on top of tuition.

upcoming_changes.fall_2026: 2% tuition increase approved for new resident undergraduates. Nonresident and international students will see ~2-3.3% increases.

components.note: These are per-semester figures from UIC's professional fee schedules. Exact amounts vary slightly by student level. CampusCare can be WAIVED if you have qualifying outside insurance — submit waiver by deadline to save $697/year. Student-to-Student and Sustainability fees may be refundable if you opt out by the deadline.

aspire_grant.what_it_is: UIC's Aspire Grant covers 100% of tuition AND mandatory fees — essentially free tuition — for eligible Illinois students.
aspire_grant.how_much: $0 tuition and fees. Does NOT automatically cover housing, food, books, or personal expenses.
who_qualifies: U.S. citizen, eligible noncitizen, or eligible undocumented student
Family income and assets of $75,000 or less on FAFSA or RISE Act application
New first-year OR new transfer student (not continuing students)
Enrolled in at least 12 credit hours each semester
aspire_grant.how_to_apply: No separate application — submit FAFSA or RISE Act Alternative Application by March 15. Apply and get admitted to UIC. Complete all financial aid requirements by the deadline.

chancellors_fellows_award.amount: $7,500/year for Illinois first-year students, $5,000/year for Illinois transfer students

requirements.must_be: Illinois resident, first-year student, 2025 Illinois high school graduate
academic_thresholds: 3.70 unweighted GPA + at least 8 honors/AP/IB Higher Level core courses across grades 11-12, OR
requirements.income_requirement: Family income at or below 8x the federal poverty guideline

chancellors_fellows_award.renewal: Must maintain 3.0 cumulative GPA and continuous full-time enrollment

presidents_award_program.requirements: Illinois student with outstanding academic achievement + at least one of: from underrepresented Illinois county/high school, first-generation student, Pell/MAP eligible
presidents_award_program.note: No exact GPA/ACT/SAT cutoff is publicly published. No separate application — reviewed automatically after admission. Honors Scholars must also apply to Honors College.

merit_tuition_award.note: No exact GPA/test cutoff published. Selection considers GPA, test scores, and selected major.

lake_county_indiana_award.purpose: Makes tuition equivalent to in-state rate for Lake County, Indiana residents

tribal_nation_tuition_award.purpose: Makes tuition equivalent to in-state rate for eligible Tribal Nation members
tribal_nation_tuition_award.note: Starting 2025, also available to graduate and professional incoming students

housing_grant_nonresident.note: May be renewable if funds are available

student_to_student_grant.who: Illinois resident undergraduates, pursuing first bachelor's degree, full-time, MAP eligible, EFC 0

snap_portal.description: UIC has 500+ scholarships in the SnAP scholarship portal. Many are college/department-specific and NOT part of the central admissions list. Check every year.
snap_portal.tip: LAS awards 30+ scholarships ranging from $400 to full year of tuition. Engineering has a large scholarship network.

statistics.percent_receiving_aid: ~65-79% of students receive some grant or scholarship
statistics.total_aid_awarded: Nearly $200 million in financial aid and scholarships per year

payment_plans.installments: Up to 6 installments for fall and spring depending on when you enroll
payment_plans.late_fees: No monthly late payment fees while enrolled in the plan

tuition_waivers.senior_citizen: Available to Illinois residents 65+ with low household income. Covers tuition only, not all fees.

graduate_assistantship.eligibility: Qualifying TA, RA, or GA appointment between 25-67% for required duration
covers: Part of CampusCare in fall/spring (not summer)

residency_for_tuition.dependent_students: Residency can change once parent/spouse/guardian establishes Illinois residency. One parent in Illinois can be enough.
residency_for_tuition.independent_students: Must live in Illinois for one FULL YEAR for reasons other than education. Cannot attend school during that year. Must show domicile and intent to stay permanently.
residency_for_tuition.f1_visa: F-1 students generally do NOT qualify for Illinois residency for tuition purposes.

niu.verdict: UIC is more expensive on direct billed cost, mainly because UIC fees are higher

uiuc.verdict: UIC is usually CHEAPER than UIUC, especially for nonresidents

siu.note: SIU charges out-of-state domestic students the in-state rate
siu.verdict: SIU can be much cheaper for out-of-state domestic students unless they win a UIC tuition award

cost_saving_tips: Illinois families under $75,000 — target Aspire first. It covers full tuition and mandatory fees (free.uic.edu)
Strong Illinois first-year students — apply for Chancellor's Fellows by November 3. One of the few awards with a clear published threshold worth $7,500/year
Submit FAFSA or RISE by March 15 for Aspire eligibility. Missing this deadline could cost you everything
Out-of-state students — look at Merit Tuition Award, Lake County Indiana, and Tribal Nation awards to drastically cut the nonresident premium
Waive CampusCare if you have qualifying outside insurance — saves ~$697/year
Use the payment plan (Nelnet) if cash flow is the issue — spread charges over 6 installments
Off-campus housing ($14,400) is cheaper than on-campus ($16,500) but factor in commuting costs
Finish within 4 years — tuition is guaranteed by cohort. Extending enrollment exposes you to newer, higher rates
Check SnAP scholarship portal every year for 500+ scholarships, many not on the main admissions list
Opt out of Student-to-Student Fee ($3) and Sustainability Fee ($6) by deadline if you want a refund

quick_answers.how_much_does_uic_cost_in_state: For 2025-2026, in-state undergrad direct billed cost is ~$16,314/year (tuition + fees). Add housing (~$16,500 on campus) and other expenses for a total COA of ~$40,310.
quick_answers.how_much_does_uic_cost_out_of_state: ~$58,512 total estimated COA for 2025-2026. But UIC offers several nonresident awards that can significantly reduce this.
quick_answers.is_uic_free_for_illinois_students: It can be! The Aspire Grant covers 100% of tuition and fees for Illinois students from families earning $75,000 or less. Visit free.uic.edu
quick_answers.how_does_uic_compare_to_uiuc: UIC is generally cheaper than UIUC, especially for out-of-state students.
quick_answers.what_gpa_do_i_need_for_merit_scholarship: For Chancellor's Fellows: 3.70 GPA + ACT 33/SAT 1450 OR 3.70 GPA + 8 honors/AP courses. For other awards, UIC doesn't publish exact cutoffs.
quick_answers.average_student_debt: 43% of UIC graduates borrow, with an average of $21,381 in cumulative debt.
